what_it_does: Declares a held substrate decision record that defines how the company's highest-volume live data streams (running log, marks, notes, pins) should be addressable, offering first-class, references-only, and mixed options and recommending a mix.
observations: It is a decision record under decisions/ with id event-streams and scope global.
It frames an addressability trade-off for the highest-volume live data: every event individually pointable, only indirect references, or a mix.
The mix option is flagged recommended: true, keeping significant items first-class and the raw high-volume log reference-only.
It resolves decision://global/event-streams and cites explanation_source code://build-prep/the-one-application/UNION-DIVERGENCE-LEDGER.md#TH2.
It includes legibility metadata (name, is, why) describing the decision in plain language.
The docstring notes it is a held substrate decision meant to render after substrate-home, owner/read attributed to Tim, and override-friendly.
The file contains no executable code, functions, or classes; only a DECISION dictionary literal.
standouts: The recommended option is a mixed addressability model: decisions, marks, and notes first-class; raw high-volume stream reference-only.
It is explicitly a held substrate decision intended to render after substrate-home and resolve a decision:// URI.
It embeds editorial voice about whose read it is and that it is override-friendly.
conventions: Follows a decision-record shape with id, meaning, options, scope, subtype, explanation_source, and legibility block.
Uses a module-level docstring to state provenance, intent, and governance context.
Breaks typical Python module conventions by containing only a data literal and no code symbols.
Marks the recommended option with a boolean inside the option dict rather than in a separate field.
concerns: The recommendation rationale and owner/override notes live in prose and comments, not structured fields.
The significance rule for which stream items become first-class is left unspecified.
There is no visible schema or validation enforcing the decision-record shape.
Editorial phrasing in the docstring (My read, your call) may blur neutral record with personal opinion.
notes: A decision record that opts for a mixed addressability model for live event streams, making marks and notes first-class while leaving the bulk raw log reference-only.
questions: What exact criteria classify an event, mark, or note as significant enough to be first-class?
How is this decision consumed or enforced by downstream code or rendering?
What is substrate-home and how does this decision depend on or render after it?
Who is Tim in this context and how is override authority exercised?
What does resolves decision://global/event-streams mean mechanically?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to resolve decision://global/event-streams as a held substrate decision about event-stream addressability, and the file contains exactly that decision record, so purpose and content align.
apparent_themes: Addressability trade-offs for high-volume live data
Balancing expressive power against storage and naming cost
Distinguishing significant artifacts from bulk streams
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish a lightweight, reversible policy that lets people directly link to decisions, marks, and notes without paying the cost of individually naming every raw event in high-volume streams.
novelty: It encodes a high-level architectural trade-off as a first-class decision record with enumerated options, a recommended choice, a decision URI, and legibility metadata, all stored as a Python data literal.
contribution: It contributes a concrete addressability policy for live event streams, capturing the trade-off space and a recommended mixed model that a fused system could keep as a decision node.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a Python decision record defining the addressability model for the company's highest-volume live event streams, offering first-class, references-only, and mixed options and recommending that decisions, marks, and notes be first-class while the raw log remains reference-only.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to resolve decision://global/event-streams by recording a held, reversible substrate decision about how directly events, marks, and notes can be referenced.
